title: Containers stop if dependency gets updated
body:
### Issue Description

I have two podman-systemd containers:
- [photoprism](https://github.com/M1cha/homeserver/blob/8952888b45fb28016aa9561f86b4c2bdc98ada40/files/containers/photoprism.container#L1)
- [photoprism-mariadb](https://github.com/M1cha/homeserver/blob/8952888b45fb28016aa9561f86b4c2bdc98ada40/files/containers/bitwarden-mariadb.container#LL1C1-L1C1)

They both have:
- auto start enabled using `default.target`
- auto restart enabled using `Restart=always`
- auto update enabled using `Label=io.containers.autoupdate=registry`

photoprism depends on photoprism-mariadb due to:
```
After=network.target photoprism-mariadb.service
Requires=photoprism-mariadb.service
```

Now, whenever photoprism-mariadb.service gets restarted because it was updated by `podman-auto-update.service`, `photoprism` stops until I start it manually. If I run `systemctl restart photoprism-mariadb`, `photoprism` will stop as well, but restart after 10 seconds as expected.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create two containers in the given config
2. Wait for an update to the dependency


### Describe the results you received

The container stopped forever.

### Describe the results you expected

The container should restart as expected.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @M1cha!

I can reproduce.  Internally `podman auto-update` does a `stop + start` instead of a `restart` which seems to be the source of the issue.
